structured_case.title: Helix Robotics v. Carter — trade secret misappropriation by former engineer
claims: misappropriation of trade secrets (Arkansas Trade Secrets Act)
relief_sought: Preliminary and permanent injunction barring use or disclosure of misappropriated files
Compensatory damages and unjust enrichment of at least $1.2M
Exemplary damages and attorney fees under the Arkansas Trade Secrets Act
Order requiring return and forensic deletion of all confidential materials
key_facts: Defendant served as senior controls engineer from 2021 until resignation on November 1, 2025.
Forensic review of company laptop shows 4,200 files copied to a personal USB drive in the 14 days before resignation.
Files include proprietary CAD drawings and motor-tuning parameters protected by access controls and an NDA signed at hire.
Defendant joined a direct competitor on November 7, 2025 and demonstrated functionally identical robot behavior at a December trade show.

contract_clauses.name: Confidentiality
contract_clauses.text: Employee shall hold all Confidential Information in strict confidence and shall not use such information for any purpose other than performance of duties for the Company.

contract_clauses.name: Return of materials
contract_clauses.text: Upon termination, Employee shall promptly return all Company property and permanently delete any Confidential Information from personal devices.

cases.name: Saline Valley Tech v. Reeves
cases.holding_summary: Bulk file transfer to personal media in the days before resignation, coupled with employment by a competitor, supports a strong likelihood of misappropriation justifying preliminary injunctive relief.

cases.name: Ozark Diagnostics v. Lin
cases.holding_summary: Reasonable measures to maintain secrecy include access controls, NDAs, and exit interviews; their existence supports trade-secret status.

cases.name: Davis v. Ozark Manufacturing
cases.holding_summary: Termination without cause during a definite employment term breaches the contract; damages are measured by wages for the unexpired term minus mitigation earnings.

cases.name: Delta Foundry v. Pratt
cases.holding_summary: General skill and knowledge acquired in employment are not trade secrets; only protectable information may form the basis of misappropriation.

favorable_precedents.name: Saline Valley Tech v. Reeves
favorable_precedents.holding_summary: Bulk file transfer to personal media in the days before resignation, coupled with employment by a competitor, supports a strong likelihood of misappropriation justifying preliminary injunctive relief.

favorable_precedents.name: Ozark Diagnostics v. Lin
favorable_precedents.holding_summary: Reasonable measures to maintain secrecy include access controls, NDAs, and exit interviews; their existence supports trade-secret status.

unfavorable_precedents.name: Delta Foundry v. Pratt
unfavorable_precedents.holding_summary: General skill and knowledge acquired in employment are not trade secrets; only protectable information may form the basis of misappropriation.

predicted_outcomes.methodology: Bayesian-style estimate from CAP precedent sample plaintiff outcomes, adjusted for party role and precedent depth.

scenarios.description: Preliminary injunction granted; merits trial proceeds on damages and exemplary award.
Full permanent injunction, $1.2M+ damages, exemplary award, and fees under the Arkansas Trade Secrets Act.
Court limits relief to NDA enforcement; trade-secret claim narrowed under Delta Foundry's general-skill principle.

predicted_outcomes.likely_judgment_summary: Preliminary injunction granted; merits trial proceeds on damages and exemplary award.

win_strategies.title: Move immediately for preliminary injunction and forensic preservation
actions: File TRO/PI motion with forensic declaration showing the 4,200-file USB transfer.
Request expedited discovery and a forensic-imaging protocol for defendant's devices.
win_strategies.rationale: Saline Valley supports early injunctive relief on bulk-copy facts.

win_strategies.title: Document reasonable measures to maintain secrecy
actions: Catalog access controls, NDAs, training records, and exit-interview practices.
Tie each protected file to its access-control list and classification label.
win_strategies.rationale: Ozark Diagnostics treats demonstrable measures as central to trade-secret status.

win_strategies.title: Distinguish general skill from protectable information
actions: Identify the precise CAD drawings and tuning parameters at issue.
Pre-empt Delta Foundry arguments with declarations on independent economic value.

win_strategies.title: Quantify damages and unjust enrichment
actions: Retain an industry expert to value the misappropriated R&D and head-start advantage.
Subpoena competitor's sales and marketing materials post-Nov. 7, 2025.
win_strategies.rationale: Trade-secret damages can combine actual loss with unjust enrichment under the Act.

win_strategies.title: Position for exemplary damages and fees
actions: Plead willful and malicious misappropriation with particularity.
Preserve evidence of defendant's destruction or concealment efforts.
win_strategies.rationale: Willful misconduct unlocks exemplary damages and fee-shifting under the statute.

favorable_judgment_tactics.detail: Lead with the preliminary-injunction record to define the merits narrative.
Anchor the case to specific files, controls, and a single resignation timeline.
Pair injunctive relief with damages framework to avoid one-or-the-other framing.
Use injunctive leverage to negotiate a structured settlement and return-of-materials protocol.

complaint_outline.content: Helix Robotics LLC v. Tyler Carter
Plaintiff: Helix Robotics LLC; Defendant: Tyler Carter
misappropriation of trade secrets; breach of confidentiality; breach of duty of loyalty
Particularized allegations tying each protected category to access controls, the USB transfer, and competitor use.
Preliminary and permanent injunction; damages and unjust enrichment of ≥ $1.2M; exemplary damages and fees; return-and-deletion order

proposed_exhibits.description: Forensic declaration with USB transfer timeline
Access-control logs for the protected file repositories
Trade-show video and competitor product materials
Key precedent: 320 Ark. 415 (1995) Saline Valley Tech v. Reeves

pre_filing_checklist: Confirm three-year statute of limitations under the Arkansas Trade Secrets Act.
Prepare TRO/PI papers in parallel with the complaint.
Implement a litigation hold notice to defendant and his new employer.
Verify filing fee and e-filing account for Pulaski County Circuit Court.
Review formatting requirements (margins, line numbering, word limits).

steps: Open e-filing portal for Circuit Court.
Select complaint initiating civil action and attach TRO/PI motion.
Upload PDF with embedded exhibits; pay filing fee.
Serve defendant per Rule 4 / local equivalent within required period.
how_to_file.note: This system does not e-file on your behalf.

top_precedents.name: Saline Valley Tech v. Reeves
top_precedents.headnotes: Bulk file transfer to personal media in the days before resignation, coupled with employment by a competitor, supports a strong likelihood of misappropriation justifying preliminary injunctive relief.
top_precedents.snippet: The court credited forensic evidence of mass copying on the eve of departure as compelling circumstantial proof of intent to misappropriate.

top_precedents.name: Ozark Diagnostics v. Lin
top_precedents.headnotes: Reasonable measures to maintain secrecy include access controls, NDAs, and exit interviews; their existence supports trade-secret status.
top_precedents.snippet: The employer's documented secrecy program met statutory reasonable-measures threshold.
